stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • In the early 1900s, over 7,000 unique varieties of pears were grown in the US.
  • The expansion of long-distance shipping in the mid-1900s narrowed the range of pears grown for the commercial market.
  • Thousands of pear varieties considered too delicate or unprofitable for shipping were lost.
  • Today, only a few pear varieties dominate US grocery stores, making up the vast majority of pears sold.
  • The Heritage Pear Project, based in Oregon, attempts to locate and cultivate forgotten pear varieties.
stem: The student wants to emphasize the decline in unique pear varieties in the US and specify why this decline occurred. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish these goals?

answerOptions.content: The Heritage Pear Project, based in Oregon, attempts to locate and cultivate forgotten pear varieties.

Although over 7,000 unique pear varieties were grown in the US in the early 1900s, today only a few varieties dominate grocery stores—a decline largely caused by the mid-1900s expansion of long-distance shipping, which narrowed the range of pears grown for the commercial market.
Today, only a few pear varieties dominate US grocery stores, making up the vast majority of pears sold.
In the early 1900s, over 7,000 unique pear varieties were grown in the US.

rationale:
Choice B is the best answer. The sentence emphasizes the decline in unique pear varieties (over 7,000 varieties down to a few dominant ones) and specifies why the decline occurred (the mid-1900s expansion of long-distance shipping narrowed the commercial range).

Choice A is incorrect. The sentence describes a recovery effort; it neither emphasizes the decline nor specifies its cause. Choice C is incorrect. The sentence reports the current state but neither emphasizes the decline nor specifies its cause. Choice D is incorrect. The sentence describes only the earlier state of pear variety, without emphasizing the decline or its cause.
